articles.headline: The Fitter You Get, the Bigger Your Brain Boost from Every Workout
articles.body: A new UCL study published in Brain Research found that getting fitter does not just make your body stronger — it makes your brain respond better to exercise too. Researchers put inactive people through 12 weeks of cycling three times a week. As fitness improved, the brain released significantly more BDNF — a protein that grows new brain cells and strengthens connections — after each workout. Even a 15-minute session triggered the spike. The takeaway: consistency compounds. The more you show up, the smarter your brain gets from every single session.
articles.takeaway: Show up consistently. Every workout builds a bigger brain payoff for the next one.

articles.headline: You Don't Have to Walk Every Day. Just Walk Enough on the Days You Do.
articles.body: New research is quietly burying the '10,000 steps every day' rule. A study published just this week found that hitting 8,000 steps on just one or two days a week is linked to a 15% reduction in mortality risk. Separate Harvard research confirmed that walking for longer stretches — even 10 to 15 minutes at a time — beats scattered short bursts for heart health and longevity. The message is clear: consistency matters, but perfection does not. Two solid walks on your busiest week still moves the needle in a big way.
articles.takeaway: Aim for 8,000 steps on at least two days a week — even if the rest of the week is chaos.
articles.source: YourNews / Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

articles.headline: Your Gym Supplement Might Also Be Protecting Your Brain
articles.body: Creatine has long been the go-to for strength and power. Now scientists are taking a hard look at what it does above the neck. A major review published by ScienceDaily this week found that creatine may support memory, mood, and mental processing speed — particularly in older adults and people with naturally lower levels. About 5% of your body's creatine lives in your brain, where it fuels cellular energy under stress. Emerging data also suggests it can buffer the cognitive fog that comes with poor sleep. Still early, but the evidence is building fast.
articles.takeaway: If you're already taking creatine for strength, know it may be doing double duty for your brain too.

product.name: Thorne Creatine Monohydrate
product.blurb: The most researched supplement in history. Works for strength, brain health, and endurance.

articles.headline: The New Protein Target Most People Are Still Missing at Breakfast
articles.body: The 2025-2030 Dietary Guidelines for Americans, analyzed in a new Journal of Nutrition paper, now recommend 1.2 to 1.6 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight daily — well above the old standard. For a 175-pound person, that is around 95 to 130 grams per day. The catch? Most Americans load up at dinner and skip protein at breakfast, crossing the muscle-building threshold only once or twice a day. Research shows you need 25 to 40 grams of quality protein per meal to actually trigger muscle protein synthesis — not just hit a daily total.
articles.takeaway: Front-load your protein. Aim for 30 grams at breakfast, not just dinner.

quote.text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
quote.author: Aristotle
quote.context: Ancient Greek philosopher whose ideas on habit and virtue still underpin modern behavioral science.

workout.title: Press & Burn: Monday Push
workout.warmup: Spend 3 minutes loosening up: 10 arm circles each direction, 10 shoulder rolls, and 15 slow bodyweight squats to wake up the posterior chain. Finish with 10 cat-cow stretches to open the chest.

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.notes: Hands just outside shoulder width, core tight, full range of motion. Drop to knees if you need to keep quality high.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
exercises.notes: Hips high in a pike position, lower your head toward the floor. This targets the shoulders. The higher your hips, the harder it gets.

exercises.name: Wide Push-Up
exercises.notes: Hands wider than shoulders to emphasize the chest. Focus on squeezing the chest at the top of each rep.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.notes: Hands together under your chest, elbows track back, not out. These are tough. Scale to a decline or incline surface as needed.

exercises.name: Dip (Chair or Bench)
exercises.notes: Hands on a sturdy chair or bench behind you, lower until your arms hit 90 degrees. Lean slightly forward to hit the chest more. Keep your feet flat on the floor.

workout.cooldown: Finish with a 90-second chest doorframe stretch on each side and 60 seconds of slow deep breathing to bring your heart rate down. Roll out the shoulders if you have a foam roller handy.

product.name: TriggerPoint GRID Foam Roller
product.blurb: Five minutes of rolling saves hours of stiffness. Non-negotiable on hard training days.

meal.title: Cottage Cheese Power Bowl with Smoked Salmon and Everything Bagel Seasoning
ingredients: 1 slice whole grain toast or 2 rice cakes
meal.instructions: Scoop cottage cheese into a bowl. Layer smoked salmon on top. Add avocado slices and cherry tomatoes. Sprinkle generously with everything bagel seasoning and a squeeze of lemon. Serve with toast or rice cakes on the side.
meal.tip: Make a double batch of the base the night before — cottage cheese and seasoning keep overnight in the fridge. Just add the fresh toppings in the morning and you are out the door in 3 minutes.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
